senses.definition: Designed to counteract or protect against harmful mists or vapors, especially bad air thought to cause disease.
examples: Nineteenth‑century doctors sometimes recommended antimephitic remedies for patients living near swamps.
The pamphlet advertised an antimephitic powder to purify the foul air in crowded houses.

senses.definition: One of the repeated parts or segments that are arranged around a central axis in a radially symmetrical organism.
examples: Each antimere of the starfish contains a similar set of organs.
In some simple animals, damage to one antimere can be compensated by the others.

senses.definition: Plural of antimere: the repeated parts or segments arranged around a central axis in a radially symmetrical organism.
examples: The organism’s body plan consists of five antimeres around the central mouth.
In echinoderms, the antimeres are often nearly identical in structure.

senses.definition: Opposing or intended to block the merging of companies or organizations.
examples: The government adopted strong antimerger policies to protect competition.
Antimerger regulations can prevent large firms from gaining excessive market power.

senses.definition: A person or group that opposes mergers between companies or organizations.
examples: Antimerger activists argued that the deal would hurt consumers.
The proposal faced strong criticism from antimerger groups.

senses.definition: Relating to preventing or opposing the merging of companies or organizations.
examples: The new antimerging rules restrict large acquisitions in the sector.
Antimerging efforts increased after several high‑profile corporate takeovers.

senses.definition: Relating to or consisting of antimeres in a radially symmetrical organism.
examples: The antimeric structure of the organism makes regeneration possible.
Researchers mapped the antimeric arrangement of the sea urchin’s body parts.

senses.definition: A subtribe of the Malagasy people living in the central‑eastern region of Madagascar.
examples: The Antimerina played an important role in the island’s pre‑colonial history.
Anthropologists have studied the traditional customs of the Antimerina.

senses.definition: The condition of a body or structure being divided into several similar parts arranged around a central axis.
examples: Antimerism is a key feature of many radially symmetrical animals.
The degree of antimerism can vary between related species.

senses.definition: Plural of antimerism: different instances or types of structures divided into similar parts around a central axis.
examples: Various antimerisms can be observed in the phylum.
The textbook compares the antimerisms found in jellyfish and sea stars.

senses.definition: Plant tissue that produces substances slowing or preventing the formation of new meristematic (growing) tissue.
examples: Researchers isolated an antimeristem to study how it controls root growth.
The presence of an antimeristem may explain the plant’s limited branching.

senses.definition: The occurrence of opposite or contrasting characteristics in different parts of an organism.
examples: Antimesia can be seen in the contrasting colors on the insect’s body.
The study examined antimesia between the dorsal and ventral surfaces of the animal.

senses.definition: A subatomic particle that is the antiparticle of a meson and has the same mass as the meson but opposite charge or quantum numbers.
examples: The collision produced a meson and an antimeson that quickly annihilated each other.
Physicists detected the antimeson in the high‑energy experiment.

senses.definition: A rhetorical device in which a phrase or clause is repeated with the words in reverse order to create emphasis or contrast.
examples: The famous line "Ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country" is an antimetabole.
Writers often use antimetabole to make their message more memorable.

senses.definition: Plural of antimetabole: repeated phrases or clauses where the words are reversed to create emphasis or contrast.
examples: The speech is full of antimetaboles that highlight its main themes.
Students were asked to identify antimetaboles in famous political speeches.

senses.definition: Relating to or acting as an antimetabolite that interferes with normal metabolic processes.
examples: The drug has strong antimetabolic effects on rapidly dividing cells.
Researchers are developing new antimetabolic therapies for cancer treatment.

senses.definition: Drugs or substances that act as antimetabolites and interfere with normal metabolic processes, especially in cells that divide quickly.
examples: Certain antimetabolics are used in chemotherapy to slow tumor growth.
The patient received a combination of antimetabolics and radiation therapy.

senses.definition: A substance that is similar to a normal body chemical and blocks its action, especially one used as a drug to stop cells from growing or dividing.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antimetabolite to treat the patient’s leukemia.
Antimetabolite therapy can cause side effects such as hair loss and nausea.

senses.definition: Drugs or substances that block normal metabolic processes, especially by imitating natural molecules and preventing cell growth.
examples: Antimetabolites are commonly used in cancer treatment to slow the spread of tumors.
The combination of antimetabolites increased the effectiveness of the therapy.

senses.definition: Opposed to or rejecting metaphysical explanations, especially those that go beyond observable reality.
examples: His antimetaphysical approach focused only on what could be scientifically tested.
The movement was strongly antimetaphysical and distrusted abstract speculation.

senses.definition: Plural of antimetathesis: rhetorical figures that reverse the order of words or phrases to create contrast or emphasis.
examples: The poem contains several antimetatheses that underline its central opposition.
Students analyzed the antimetatheses used in classical speeches.

senses.definition: A rhetorical device in which words or phrases are repeated in reverse order to highlight contrast or create emphasis.
examples: The orator used antimetathesis to balance the two sides of his argument.
Antimetathesis can make a sentence sound more striking and memorable.

senses.definition: Relating to or using antimetathesis, especially reversing words or ideas for effect.
examples: The passage has an antimetathetic structure that contrasts hope and fear.
His antimetathetic phrasing made the slogan easy to remember.

senses.definition: A device for measuring very small amounts or for detecting very small changes in a quantity.
examples: The engineer used an antimeter to detect tiny changes in voltage.
An antimeter can register extremely small variations in pressure.

senses.definition: A style of working that rejects fixed rules and systems and instead relies on freedom, spontaneity, or reaction against usual methods.
examples: The director adopted an antimethod that allowed actors to improvise most scenes.
Some artists see antimethod as a way to escape rigid academic training.

senses.definition: Rejecting or not following fixed methods, especially as a deliberate choice.
examples: Her antimethodic teaching style encouraged students to explore on their own.
The workshop took an antimethodic approach to problem‑solving.

senses.definition: Deliberately not careful and step‑by‑step in method, often as a reaction against strict systems.
examples: The artist’s antimethodical process produced surprising results.
He took an antimethodical route to writing, ignoring outlines and plans.

senses.definition: In a way that avoids fixed methods or careful step‑by‑step planning.
examples: She worked antimethodically, following intuition rather than a plan.
The tasks were completed antimethodically and in no clear order.

senses.definition: The quality of avoiding fixed methods or careful, step‑by‑step organization.
examples: His antimethodicalness frustrated colleagues who preferred clear procedures.
The group’s success was surprising given their antimethodicalness.

senses.definition: Not following regular meter, especially in poetry or music, often on purpose.
examples: The poem’s antimetrical lines create a feeling of unease.
Her antimetrical style rejected the traditional rhythm of the sonnet.

senses.definition: A condition in which the two eyes have different types of refractive error, for example one is short‑sighted and the other is long‑sighted.
examples: The optometrist diagnosed the patient with antimetropia.
Antimetropia can make it difficult for the eyes to work together comfortably.

senses.definition: Having different types of refractive error in the two eyes.
examples: Her antimetropic vision required specially designed lenses.
Antimetropic patients may experience headaches when reading.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or protect against miasma, that is, bad air once believed to cause disease.
examples: Victorian cities invested in antimiasmatic measures such as sewers and better ventilation.
Doctors promoted antimiasmatic practices to reduce the spread of illness.

senses.definition: Able to kill or stop the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria, viruses, or fungi that can cause disease.
examples: This soap has antimicrobial properties and helps reduce the spread of germs.
Doctors are worried about bacteria becoming resistant to antimicrobial drugs.

senses.definition: A substance, especially a drug, that kills or slows the growth of microorganisms.
examples: The infection was treated with a broad‑spectrum antimicrobial.
Overuse of antimicrobials can lead to resistant strains of bacteria.

senses.definition: Substances or drugs that kill or slow the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria or fungi.
examples: Hospitals carefully control the use of antimicrobials to limit resistance.
New antimicrobials are needed to treat drug‑resistant infections.

senses.definition: Able to kill or stop the growth of microorganisms; antimicrobial.
examples: The solution has strong antimicrobic action.
Antimicrobic agents are used to disinfect medical instruments.

senses.definition: The belief or political position that opposes strong military influence, high military spending, or the use of military force to solve problems.
examples: His antimilitarism led him to campaign against arms spending.
Antimilitarism is a central value of many peace movements.

senses.definition: Different forms or expressions of opposition to military power or influence.
examples: The book compares various antimilitarisms across Europe in the twentieth century.
Religious and secular antimilitarisms sometimes share common goals.

senses.definition: A person who opposes strong military influence, high military spending, or the use of military force when it can be avoided.
examples: As an antimilitarist, she joined protests against the new weapons program.
Many antimilitarists support non‑violent solutions to conflict.

senses.definition: Opposed to strong military influence or high military spending.
examples: The party’s antimilitarist platform appealed to young voters.
He wrote an antimilitarist essay criticizing compulsory service.

senses.definition: Opposed to the idea that strong armed forces are necessary or should be used to gain political or economic advantage.
examples: The organization has a clearly antimilitaristic message.
Her antimilitaristic views developed after seeing the effects of war.

senses.definition: In a way that shows opposition to strong military influence or high military spending.
examples: She spoke antimilitaristically about the government’s defense budget.
The play is antimilitaristically critical of national pride based on war.

senses.definition: People who oppose strong military influence, high military spending, or the use of military force when it can be avoided.
examples: Antimilitarists organized a protest against the new military base.
Many antimilitarists support international cooperation and diplomacy.

senses.definition: Opposed to the military or to military ideas, activities, or influence.
examples: The protesters shouted antimilitary slogans outside the base.
His antimilitary attitude made him unpopular with some voters.

senses.definition: Designed to detect, resist, or protect against landmines or other explosive mines.
examples: The vehicle is equipped with an antimine system to protect soldiers.
Antimine technology has improved road safety in former war zones.

senses.definition: Opposed to a government minister or to the policies of government ministers.
examples: The antiministerial tone of the article angered officials.
Several antiministerial protests were held in the capital.

senses.definition: A person who opposes government ministers or their policies.
examples: Antiministerialists criticized the cabinet’s handling of the crisis.
He became known as an antiministerialist after leading the campaign.

senses.definition: Having views that oppose government ministers or their policies.
examples: The newspaper adopted an antiministerialist line.
Antiministerialist feelings were strong among the workers.

senses.definition: In a way that opposes government ministers or their policies.
examples: The columnist wrote antiministerially about the new law.
He spoke antiministerially during the debate in parliament.

senses.definition: Plural of antiminsion, a consecrated cloth in Eastern Orthodox churches on which the Eucharist is celebrated.
examples: The bishop blessed new antiminsia for the growing parish.
Historic antiminsia are preserved in the monastery’s treasury.

senses.definition: In the Eastern Orthodox Church, a consecrated cloth containing relics, on which the Eucharist must be celebrated.
examples: The priest carefully unfolded the antiminsion before the liturgy.
Without an antiminsion, the Eucharist cannot be properly celebrated in that tradition.

senses.definition: Opposing marriage or sexual relationships between people considered to be of different races.
examples: Antimiscegenation laws once banned interracial marriages in many places.
The politician’s antimiscegenation views were widely condemned as racist.

senses.definition: Designed to detect, track, or destroy incoming missiles.
examples: The country invested heavily in an antimissile defense system.
Antimissile technology has advanced rapidly in recent years.

senses.definition: A missile or device used to destroy incoming missiles.
examples: The antimissile was launched to intercept the incoming rocket.
Antimissiles form an important part of the country’s defense system.
